title: Sentinel Shield external-adopter session record
description: Evidence produced by tests/adopter/black-box-install.sh: a machine-readable transcript of a first-time external adopter driving the DOCUMENTED install flow (acquire -> verify -> dry-run -> install -> doctor -> local pipeline) from an empty workspace. Determinism comes from OBSERVABLE, CONSTRAINED execution: every engine command runs under a minimal allowlisted environment (env -i with only PATH/HOME/TMPDIR plus the documented SENTINEL_SHIELD_REF/SENTINEL_SHIELD_PATH when set) with stdin from /dev/null, so any undocumented prerequisite or interactive prompt manifests as a FAILED step. Every command, exit code, elapsed time, generated file and user-facing message is recorded. It carries NO secrets and NO absolute local paths (HOME relativized to '~', the workspace root to '<workspace>', the target to '<target>'). result=pass only when every required step succeeded under the constrained environment.

harness.description: Which adopter harness produced the record. 'black-box-install' is the single-session harness; 'adopter-scenarios' is the multi-environment suite that emits one record per scenario.

scenario.description: Optional scenario id when produced by the multi-environment suite (e.g. clean-linux|minimal-posix|managed-file-conflict|read-only-project|interrupted-recovery|proxy-configured|offline-restricted). Absent for the single black-box session.

platform.description: Optional redacted host descriptor the scenario ran on (e.g. the output of `uname -s`), so macOS-vs-Linux shell coverage is attributable.

budget_seconds.description: Optional per-step wall-clock budget (seconds) the scorecard's bounded-durations criterion holds this scenario's mandatory steps to. When absent, the scorecard applies its default budget.

recovery.description: Optional recovery evidence for scenarios that inject a failure and then recover. 'required' marks a mutation-bearing failure that MUST be reversible; 'restored' asserts the target was returned to its expected pre-failure state. The scorecard fails closed when required=true but restored!=true.

required.description: true when a mutation-bearing failure was injected that must be recoverable.

performed.description: true when a recovery operation was actually executed.

restored.description: true when recovery restored the target to its expected state (verified, not assumed).

method.description: Redacted description of how recovery was performed (e.g. 'recover-operation.sh --resume-rollback').

started_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC time the session started.

finished_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC time the session finished.

workspace.description: Redacted label for the empty workspace the session ran in (e.g. '<workspace>').

engine_source.description: How the engine was acquired for the session (redacted).

documented_inputs.description: The ONLY inputs the flow was allowed to use: documented env vars and the published docs it followed.

steps.description: Ordered transcript of the flow's steps.

command.description: The exact command run, redacted (no secrets, no absolute paths).

exit_code.description: Process exit code, or null when the step was skipped before running.

elapsed_seconds.description: Wall-clock seconds the command took, or null when skipped.

status.description: ok=ran and produced an acceptable outcome; skip=deliberately not run (explicit reason recorded); fail=required step failed.

message.description: Redacted user-facing message or skip/failure reason. A skip MUST carry an explicit reason (a skip is not a pass).

next_action.description: Redacted safe next action the adopter should take. The scorecard's errors-have-next-actions criterion requires every 'fail' step to carry a non-empty next_action.

generated_files.description: Redacted/relativized paths of files the step created.

documented_environment.description: The environment allowlist applied to every engine command (env -i base PATH/HOME/TMPDIR plus the documented SENTINEL_SHIELD_REF/SENTINEL_SHIELD_PATH when set). Any other variable was absent.

injected_inputs.description: Inputs the harness fed to engine commands' stdin. Empty: stdin is /dev/null, so no answers are supplied and a blocking read gets EOF and fails.

unexpected_prompt.description: true if an engine command tried to prompt interactively (observed on a failing step whose stdin was /dev/null); false otherwise.

result.description: pass only when every required step is ok (or an explicitly-reasoned skip) under the constrained environment; fail when any required step failed.
